verses.text: Leave the first principles and 'go on unto perfection,' not laying again the foundation. Press forward. The believer is to mature, not linger at the beginning.
Leave the first principles and 'go on unto perfection,' not laying again the foundation. Press forward. The believer is to mature, not linger at the beginning.
Leave the first principles and 'go on unto perfection,' not laying again the foundation. Press forward. The believer is to mature, not linger at the beginning.
Those once enlightened who 'fall away' cannot be renewed again to repentance, crucifying the Son afresh. Solemn warning. Apostasy from known truth is fearfully dangerous.
Those once enlightened who 'fall away' cannot be renewed again to repentance, crucifying the Son afresh. Solemn warning. Apostasy from known truth is fearfully dangerous.
Those once enlightened who 'fall away' cannot be renewed again to repentance, crucifying the Son afresh. Solemn warning. Apostasy from known truth is fearfully dangerous.
The earth that drinks rain and bears fruit is blessed; that which bears thorns is 'nigh unto cursing.' Two soils. Profession is tested by its fruit.
The earth that drinks rain and bears fruit is blessed; that which bears thorns is 'nigh unto cursing.' Two soils. Profession is tested by its fruit.

We are persuaded better things of you; God will not forget your 'work and labour of love.' Be diligent followers 'of them who through faith and patience inherit the promises.' Hopeful word. Faith and patience win the promise.

We are persuaded better things of you; God will not forget your 'work and labour of love.' Be diligent followers 'of them who through faith and patience inherit the promises.' Hopeful word. Faith and patience win the promise.
God's promise to Abraham was confirmed by an oath, and 'after he had patiently endured, he obtained the promise.' Sure word. The patient believer receives what God has sworn.
God's promise to Abraham was confirmed by an oath, and 'after he had patiently endured, he obtained the promise.' Sure word. The patient believer receives what God has sworn.
God's promise to Abraham was confirmed by an oath, and 'after he had patiently endured, he obtained the promise.' Sure word. The patient believer receives what God has sworn.
We have 'a strong consolation,' a 'hope... as an anchor of the soul,' entering within the veil, where Jesus is gone before. Steadfast anchor. Our hope is fixed in heaven, where Christ has entered.
